chapter_title.ar: باب مَا جَاءَ فِي الِاثْنَيْ عَشَرَ وَ النَّصِّ عَلَيْهِمْ ( عليهم السلام )
chapter_title.en: Chapter 126 – What has come regarding the twelve and the text upon them<sup>asws</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/230)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/230)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ الِاثْنَا عَشَرَ الْإِمَامَ مِنْ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ كُلُّهُمْ مُحَدَّثٌ مِنْ وُلْدِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ وُلْدِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ ( عليه السلام ) فَرَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ عَلِيٌّ ( عليه السلام ) هُمَا الْوَالِدَانِ .

en.hubeali: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Al Hassan Bin Ubeydullah, from Al Hassan Bin Musa Al Khashhab, from Ali Bin Sama’at, from Ali Bin Al Hassan Bin Rabat, from Ibn Azina, from Zurara who said,

‘I heard Abu Ja’farasws saying: ‘The twelve Imamsasws from the Progenyasws of Muhammadsaww, all of themasws are Muhaddath (Whom the Angels discuss with) from the children of Rasool-Allahsaww and children of Aliasws Bin Abu Talibasws. Thus, Rasool-Allahsaww and Aliasws, theyasws are the two parentsasws’.
en.sarwar: 14. Abu Ali al-Ash‘ari has narrated from ‘Ubaydallah from al-Hassan ibn Musa al-Khashshab from Ali ibn Sam‘Abdallah from Ali ibn al-Husayn ibn Ribat from ’Udhayna from Zurara who has said the following. “I heard abu Ja‘far say, ‘There will be twelve Imams from the family of Muhammad and they all will be (Muhaddathun), persons to whom angles speak. They will of the descendants of the Messenger of Allah and Ali ibn abu Talib (a.s.). The Messenger of Allah and Ali are their anscesters.”
